bull_thesis: Oklo is the highest-profile pure-play on the single hardest physical bottleneck in the AI scale-out: firm, 24/7, carbon-free baseload power for hyperscale data centers. The bull case rests on (1) a credible, dated regulatory lead — DOE Nuclear Safety Design Agreement approved, NRC PDC topical report accepted in 15 days vs 30-60 typical, INL groundbreaking done (Sep 2025), Environmental Compliance Permit issued, and an accelerated DOE Reactor Pilot Program pathway targeting criticality milestones in 2026; (2) a marquee, AI-anchored demand pipeline of ~14 GW of non-binding LOIs — 12 GW with Switch, an Equinix LOI, and a 1.2 GW Meta clean-energy campus in southern Ohio (part of Meta's up-to-6.6 GW nuclear push) — validating that the demand is real and that hyperscalers will sign for power years ahead of delivery; (3) a build-own-operate model that, once deployed, generates recurring high-switching-cost PPA revenue rather than one-time reactor sales, plus two optionality legs: a near-term radioisotope business (Groves facility built in 229 days, target criticality July 4 2026, first commercial isotope contract pending, revenue potential starting 2026) and a vertically integrated HALEU/fuel-recycling position (Centrus LOI for up to 5 Aurora cores, plus access to government plutonium/down-blended uranium) that 'isn't possible across all reactor types'; (4) a fortress balance sheet — $2.5B cash + securities ($1.6B cash, $0.9B marketable), bolstered by a $1.2B ATM raise in Q1 2026 — giving multi-year runway through first power (targeted late 2027 at INL) without a solvency cliff; (5) NVIDIA / Los Alamos / INL-Prometheus AI partnerships burnishing the AI association. Tone is offensive and execution-focused ('the story has increasingly moved from strategy to execution'). If Aurora deploys roughly on schedule and even a fraction of the 14 GW LOIs convert to priced take-or-pay PPAs, the terminal-fleet DCF dwarfs today's $10.7B cap, and the stock is down ~67% from its high, meaning much of the hype premium has already been wrung out.
bear_thesis: Oklo is a $10.7B market cap on $0 revenue — its entire value is a discounted option on a fleet that does not yet exist, and the bear case is that the option is still priced for near-certain success the company cannot yet underwrite. (1) The ~14 GW pipeline is entirely NON-BINDING LOIs — expressions of interest, not signed PPAs with payment obligations or fixed pricing; conversion rate, price, and counterparty commitment are all unproven, and hyperscalers are simultaneously signing with Vistra, TerraPower, Constellation and others (Meta's deal spread across three developers). (2) Structural dilution is baked in: shares are ~174M (+~28% YoY), the quarter was funded by a $1.2B ATM, and the company guides to $350-450M/yr of investing cash burn — so each year to first power means more equity issuance and a moving per-share denominator; the $2.5B war-chest is itself a product of heavy dilution. (3) Timeline slip risk is real and has happened before — the original Aurora-2027 framing has been recalibrated, first commercial ops is now late-2027 for INL, and commercial NRC licensing for the broader fleet remains pre-submission; any slip pushes the terminal cash flows further out and compresses the option's value. (4) A chunk of the bull narrative leans on a POLITICALLY-SUPPLIED tailwind — the May-2025 executive-order DOE Reactor Pilot Program / July-4-2026 criticality mandate — which is powerful but regime-dependent and could lose momentum. (5) The CEO has been a net seller of ~$10M of personal stock while the verbal narrative is maximally bullish. (6) Competition across the SMR field (NuScale, X-energy, Kairos, TerraPower) plus the sheer first-of-a-kind engineering/regulatory execution risk of a never-before-commercially-deployed reactor design mean the probability-weighted path to a profitable fleet is wide-distributed. With no revenue, no margins and no returns, there is zero fundamental margin of safety — the only cushion is sentiment, and sentiment in pre-revenue nuclear is violently mean-reverting (the stock round-tripped from $44.88 to $193.84 inside a year).
sop_bull: ~$95-110/share. Method: probability-weighted terminal-fleet DCF + near-term optionality, whole-company (pre-revenue, no reportable segments). Powerhouse fleet (core): assume ~3-4 GW of the ~14 GW LOI pipeline converts to deployed build-own-operate capacity by the early-2030s at ~$1.5-2.0M EBITDA/MW recurring PPA economics, discounted hard for execution/timeline/dilution risk -> ~$13-16B equity value attributable today -> ~$75-92/sh on ~174M shares (bull haircuts dilution less aggressively, assuming project-finance/DOE-loan leverage funds much of the $350-450M/yr build). Isotope unit: a credible near-term radioisotope cash business (Groves online ~mid-2026, first contract pending) worth ~$1-2B -> ~$6-11/sh. Fuel/HALEU + recycling optionality (Centrus LOI for 5 cores, plutonium access): ~$1-1.5B strategic-moat value -> ~$6-9/sh. Net cash $2.5B already inside the equity. Sum ~$95-110/sh — i.e. ~55-80% upside to the recovery thesis if execution lands.
sop_bear: ~$28-40/share. Method: same whole-company option, but with severe execution/dilution haircuts. Powerhouse fleet: discount the LOIs to a low single-digit conversion probability and push first material cash flow to the 2030s; risk-adjusted equity value ~$3-4B -> ~$17-23/sh AFTER modeling 2-3 more years of ATM/equity dilution lifting share count toward ~210-240M. Isotope unit: nascent, sub-scale, contract still pending -> ~$0.5-1B -> ~$2-4/sh. Fuel optionality: still LOI-grade, minimal credit -> ~$0-1/sh. Net cash $2.5B (~$11-12/sh today, but eroding via burn). Sum ~$28-40/sh — i.e. ~35-55% DOWNSIDE if the market re-prices a pre-revenue, dilution-funded option to its cash-plus-modest-optionality floor.
sop_breakdown: Whole-company sum-of-optionality (no reportable revenue segments yet): (1) Powerhouse fleet terminal-DCF, probability-weighted ~35-45% success on ~3 GW deployed by early-2030s build-own-operate PPAs, discounted for first-of-a-kind execution + late-2027 first-power slip risk: ~$6-8B -> ~$35-46/sh. (2) Radioisotope business (Groves criticality target Jul-4-2026, first contract pending, revenue 2026): ~$1-1.5B -> ~$6-9/sh. (3) Fuel/HALEU + recycling vertical-integration optionality (Centrus LOI, govt plutonium/uranium access): ~$0.5-1B -> ~$3-6/sh. (4) Net cash $2.5B today (~$14/sh) but burning ~$430-550M/yr all-in, so credit ~$1.8-2.2B net of two years' burn -> ~$10-13/sh. Layered overlays: ATM/equity dilution (~174M shares, rising) is a per-share headwind already partly in the count; a potential DOE loan / project-finance facility would REDUCE equity dilution and is upside not in the base. Sum ~$52-62/sh — essentially in line with the ~$60.88 spot, i.e. the market is already paying close to a reasonable probability-weighted intrinsic, with the asymmetry roughly two-sided.
risk_reward: Roughly symmetric-to-slightly-unfavorable from spot ~$60.88. Upside-to-fair (base ~$57) is ~negative-to-flat; upside-to-bull (~$100) is ~+65%; downside-to-bear floor (~$34) is ~-44%. So spot sits ABOVE the base-case midpoint: ~+65% bull vs ~-44% bear, but the base case says you are not being paid a discount today — you are paying ~fair value for a wide-distribution option. Reward/risk only turns clearly favorable on a meaningful pullback toward the high-$40s/low-$50s (the net-cash-plus-isotope floor), which is exactly the 'wait for capitulation' setup.
catalyst_status: SOFT_EXTENDED. The load-bearing catalysts are (a) the DOE Reactor Pilot Program / July-4-2026 criticality milestone (driven by the May-2025 Trump executive order) and the Groves isotope facility target criticality July 4 2026 with first commercial isotope contract 'pending'; (b) Aurora-INL first commercial operations targeted LATE 2027; (c) conversion of the ~14 GW non-binding LOIs (Switch 12 GW, Meta 1.2 GW, Equinix) into binding priced PPAs; (d) a potential DOE loan / project-finance facility. As of June 2026 these are real and dated but NON-BINDING and serially forward-referenced: the LOIs carry no payment obligation, first power is 18+ months out and has slipped before, and the July-4 criticality is a test/isotope-criticality milestone tied to a single policy regime, not a commercial-revenue inflection. No hard binary, deal-fixed (ARB), or already-spent (FIRED) catalyst with locked asymmetry in the next 90 days. Verified live (June 2026 sources): Q1-2026 10-Q, World Nuclear News / NEI on the July-4-2026 criticality deadline, Utility Dive on the Meta multi-developer nuclear deals, Oklo/Centrus 8-K on the HALEU LOI.
peer_comps_note: TRUE peers within the scale-out POWER-GEN / SMR-developer layer (all live-verified June 21 2026): OKLO ~$10.66B mkt cap (price ~$60.88, ~174M sh, $0 revenue, $2.5B cash); NuScale Power (SMR) ~$4.28B (pre-revenue, utility-anchored customers, -39% YTD, 52wk $8.85-$57.42); NANO Nuclear Energy (NNE) ~$1.2-1.5B (price ~$28.40, pre-revenue microreactor designs, 52wk $18.93-$60.87). Broader power-gen comps that DO have cash flows and AI-power exposure: Vistra (VST), Constellation (CEG), Talen (TLN) and GE Vernova (GEV) — these trade on real EBITDA/earnings and are the 'pay-for-power-now' alternative hyperscalers are signing (Meta's deal spans Oklo + Vistra + TerraPower). On EVERY conventional metric OKLO is un-rankable vs the cash-flowing names (no revenue, no margins, no ROIC, no FCF) and is therefore valued purely on option/pipeline terms. Within the pre-revenue SMR cohort OKLO carries the RICHEST valuation (~2.5x NuScale, ~7-9x Nano) — a premium justified by the AI/hyperscaler LOI density (Switch/Meta/Equinix vs NuScale's utility tilt), the DOE-pathway regulatory lead, the $2.5B war-chest, and the near-term isotope revenue leg — but it also makes OKLO the most expensive way to own the SMR theme and the most exposed to a sentiment de-rate. RANK: #1 on AI-capex exposure & balance-sheet strength, #1 (most expensive) on valuation, all three tied at zero on margins/returns, OKLO #1 on pipeline GW.
role_in_scaleout: Oklo's role is at the POWER-GENERATION root of the AI build-out stack — the firm, dispatchable, carbon-free baseload that every hyperscaler campus ultimately needs and is structurally short of; its specific differentiation is the build-own-operate PPA model + vertical fuel integration aimed directly at data-center offtakers (Switch/Meta/Equinix) rather than utilities. The demand is highly durable in DIRECTION (AI power scarcity is multi-year and worsening) but Oklo's CAPTURE of it is NOT yet durable — it is pre-revenue, first power is late-2027, the pipeline is non-binding, and hyperscalers can and do buy firm power from already-operating alternatives (gas, existing nuclear, Vistra/Constellation/Talen) while Oklo builds.
consensus_delta: The street is NOT obviously looking the wrong way on Oklo — coverage is well aware this is a pre-revenue option, and the -67% drawdown from $193.84 shows the market already de-rated the peak hype. The genuine expectations-arbitrage questions are two-sided. BULL-side delta the street may underprice: (a) the radioisotope leg as a real near-term revenue stream (Groves online mid-2026, first contract pending) that partially de-binaries the story; (b) a potential DOE loan / project-finance facility that would fund the $350-450M/yr build with LEVERAGE instead of equity, sharply reducing the dilution the bears extrapolate. BEAR-side delta the bulls underprice: (a) the ~14 GW pipeline is treated rhetorically as 'backlog' when it is non-binding LOIs with no price or payment obligation, and hyperscalers are explicitly multi-sourcing (Meta across Oklo/Vistra/TerraPower); (b) the per-share denominator is a moving target — at ~174M shares and rising via ATM, terminal-fleet value per share is being continuously diluted, so even a 'right' fleet-EBITDA call can disappoint on a per-share basis; (c) the politically-supplied DOE/July-4 tailwind is regime-dependent. Net: this is not a clean 'Wall Street is offside' mispricing — it is a fairly-priced wide-distribution option where the asymmetry is roughly two-sided at spot.
valley_of_death: Near-term (3-9 month) hazards: (1) Continued ZERO-revenue prints with a widening loss (Q1-2026 net loss $33.1M, op loss $51.2M) and 2026 guide of $80-100M operating + $350-450M investing cash use — every quarter is a burn quarter with no offsetting top line, and the isotope revenue, even if it starts in 2026, will be immaterial vs the $10.7B cap. (2) Dilution overhang: the $1.2B Q1 ATM raise signals the company will keep tapping equity; any further ATM/secondary is a direct, mechanical per-share headwind and a sentiment negative. (3) Milestone-slip risk: the July-4-2026 criticality / Groves timeline and the late-2027 first-power date are the visible checkpoints — a slip on either (first-of-a-kind nuclear timelines slip routinely) would puncture the 'strategy-to-execution' narrative. (4) Macro/positioning: Oklo is a high-beta, retail-heavy, AI-capex-correlated momentum name (round-tripped $45->$194->$61 in a year); under the current regime's stretched positioning and AI-capex-as-load-bearing-wall risk, any hyperscaler capex-guide-down or broad de-grossing event hits Oklo harder than the cash-flowing power names. The thesis can be cut in half by sentiment well before any operational catalyst arrives.
positioning_washout: Oklo's shareholder base is heavily retail + momentum/thematic (the Sam Altman halo, the 'AI nuclear' meme, a 52-week round-trip from $44.88 to $193.84 to ~$61). This is a fast-money, sentiment-driven float, NOT a yield/passive base that liquidates on a dividend cut (there is no dividend). The mechanical-selling risk is the inverse: momentum/CTA and retail capitulation on any milestone slip, equity raise, or AI-capex scare could flush the stock toward its net-cash-plus-isotope floor (high-$40s/low-$50s) in a sharp, non-fundamental move — which is precisely the capitulation entry the verdict waits for. There is no forced-seller catalyst from an index/yield mandate; the washout, if it comes, is a sentiment de-gross, and it would be the BUYING opportunity rather than a thesis-killer (the $2.5B cash means it is not a solvency flush).
forcing_function: The hardest dated re-rating triggers: (1) JULY 4, 2026 — the DOE Reactor Pilot Program criticality milestone and the Groves isotope facility target criticality; a hit (or a credible first commercial isotope contract signing) would be a tangible 'execution' proof-point; a miss/slip would puncture the narrative. (2) The conversion of any of the ~14 GW LOIs (Switch 12 GW / Meta 1.2 GW / Equinix) into a BINDING, priced PPA — this is the single event that would convert 'interest' into 'backlog' and force the street to re-underwrite revenue. (3) Announcement of a DOE loan / project-finance facility — would de-risk the dilution path and is the cleanest positive surprise. (4) Late-2027 Aurora-INL first commercial operations — the ultimate forcing function, but outside the near-term window. Of these, (1) is the nearest hard date and (2) is the highest-impact but undated.
moderator_conclusion: OKLO is a real, well-funded, best-in-class pure-play on the most fundamental physical bottleneck in the AI scale-out — firm, carbon-free, 24/7 baseload power — and it sits on the RIGHT side of every secular wave (energy-transition winner, AI-power scarcity), with no terminal-value erosion risk; the moat is NARROW-and-prospective but genuinely WIDENING (DOE/NRC regulatory lead, vertical fuel integration, build-own-operate recurring-PPA model). That earns it a place in the universe. But on the regime overlay and the expectations-arbitrage math it is a WATCH, not a buy at spot. Three things govern the call. First, the catalysts are SOFT_EXTENDED: a ~14 GW pipeline that is entirely non-binding LOIs, a first-power date 18+ months out that has slipped before, and a politically-supplied July-4 criticality milestone — there is no hard-dated, deal-fixed asymmetry in the favorable 90-day window, so the regime says DO NOT reward it as an imminent catalyst. Second, the SoP says the market is already paying ~fair value: base-case ~$57 vs spot ~$60.88, with ~+65% to the bull and ~-44% to the bear — roughly two-sided, no margin of safety, and within the pre-revenue SMR cohort OKLO is the MOST expensive way to own the theme (~2.5x NuScale, ~7-9x Nano). Third — book-independence — OKLO's entire thesis hinges on the SAME single shared macro factor (AI-capex durability) as the rest of Basket E and much of the index; in a regime where AI-capex is the load-bearing wall and positioning is stretched, concentrating in a zero-revenue, high-beta expression of that one factor is exactly the dependency §1.7 warns against. Conclusion: VERDICT B (watchlist-for-capitulation), conviction 3. This is a name to own on a sentiment washout toward the high-$40s/low-$50s net-cash-plus-isotope floor (the most likely path given retail/momentum ownership and the relentless dilution), NOT at full freight today. value_conviction 2: stripped of the AI/catalyst overlay, you are paying ~fair-to-rich for a pre-revenue option with no margin of safety, continuous dilution, and a moving per-share denominator — the −67% drawdown is a de-hype print, not a value print, so optical 'it's down a lot' is not value here.
interrogator_dossier:
# OKLO (Oklo Inc.) — Interrogator Forensic Dossier

*Prepared by the Interrogator (Claude Opus 4.8) for the investment committee. Scale-out Basket E (POWER-GEN). All figures cross-referenced against the most recent Q1 2026 earnings call (reported May 12, 2026), the Q1 2026 10-Q, and live market data as of June 21, 2026 (price ~$60.88, market cap ~$10.66B, ~174M diluted shares, down ~67% from the $193.84 52-week high).*

> **Evidence note:** Oklo is a **pre-revenue** company. There is no revenue, margin, ROIC, FCF or backlog-conversion trajectory to cross-reference in the conventional sense. The "financials" here are a cash-runway-and-dilution story, and the "narrative" is a regulatory/commercial-pipeline story. The forensic test is therefore reframed: do management's milestone claims map to *verifiable, dated* regulatory and customer events, and is the cash bridge to first revenue credible without ruinous dilution?

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

Oklo's story has migrated decisively from **"strategy" to "execution"** over the trailing year — management's own framing on the Q1 2026 call: *"the story has increasingly moved from strategy to execution."* The arc: De-SPAC era (mid-2024, via AltC / Sam Altman) was a single-product story — the Aurora microreactor on a build-own-operate utility model. 2025 broadened from one reactor to THREE business units — powerhouses (the Aurora fleet), fuel recycling/HALEU, and a near-term radioisotope business (Atomic Alchemy) introduced to pull revenue forward into 2026. The 2026 AI-pivot overlay made AI data-center power demand the dominant new driver: the LOI pipeline ballooned to ~14 GW (12 GW Switch, Equinix LOI, 1.2 GW Meta Ohio campus), with NVIDIA/Los Alamos/INL-Prometheus partnerships layered on as 'strategic enablers.' Growth driver introduced and load-bearing: AI-capex demand for firm 24/7 clean power. Quietly de-emphasized: the original simple sell-power-via-PPA utility model — it is now a far more capital-intensive, vertically integrated build-out needing $350-450M of investing cash in 2026 alone.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

Management's 'moved from strategy to execution' is substantiated on PROCESS milestones (INL groundbreaking Sep-2025; Groves built in 229 days; NRC PDC topical report accepted in 15 days vs 30-60 typical; DOE Nuclear Safety Design Agreement approved). The ~14 GW pipeline is RICHER than the numbers justify — 12 GW Switch + Equinix + 1.2 GW Meta are all non-binding LOIs with no payment obligation; zero revenue today. 'Revenue potential starting 2026' (isotopes) is UNPROVEN — first commercial isotope contract is 'pending,' Groves target criticality July 4 2026. Centrus HALEU LOI (up to 5 Aurora cores, 'timescales shift to the left for the first time') is plausible/improving but LOI-grade. Liquidity CONFIRMED strong: $2.5B cash+securities ($1.6B cash, $0.9B marketable), helped by a $1.2B Q1 ATM raise — but achieved via heavy issuance (~174M shares, +~28% YoY). Hard numbers: $0 revenue; Q1 net loss $33.1M; operating loss $51.2M; EPS -$0.19 (in line). 2026 guide $80-100M operating + $350-450M investing. The narrative is a multi-year option on a fleet that does not yet exist; the financials are a well-funded but accelerating burn with no top line.

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY

Rising / offensive. CEO DeWitte is consistently forward-leaning: 'Nuclear doesn't have to be slow...expensive, can be done in different ways'; 'incredible momentum'; 'deploy faster and do more simultaneously.' Tone has grown MORE offensive over the year. No defensive/cost/macro pivot — if anything the risk is the opposite: tone running ahead of contracted cash flows. Counter-signal: the CEO has been a NET SELLER of ~$10M of personal stock over 12 months.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY

Oklo gives milestone guidance, not financial guidance. On milestones the delivery record is Medium-to-High (INL groundbreaking, fast Groves build, accelerated NRC acceptances, DOE OTA/NSDA approvals). BUT the timeline has slipped before — the original 'Aurora 2027' framing has been recalibrated to late-2027 first commercial ops at INL, and full NRC commercial licensing for the fleet remains pre-submission. The one cash-flow item they guide (op/investing cash use) tracked in range in Q1. Reliability: MEDIUM — process milestones credible; commercial-revenue and first-power dates carry real slip risk and have moved before.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Softened rather than resolved: (a) 'when does revenue actually arrive and how big' — answered 'isotopes 2026 / first power late-2027' but magnitude vs a $10.7B cap left vague; (b) LOI-to-binding-PPA conversion — cited as momentum, which convert at what price stays qualitative; (c) dilution — the $1.2B ATM funded the quarter but the $350-450M/yr burn implies continued issuance, framed as balance-sheet strength without owning the per-share dilution path; (d) NRC vs DOE pathway — the DOE pilot pathway is presented as an accelerant but sidesteps the harder commercial NRC license, still pre-submission for the fleet.

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: $2.5B cash war-chest (multi-year runway, no near-term solvency risk); genuine dated regulatory acceleration (DOE NSDA, NRC PDC acceptance, INL groundbreaking, Environmental Compliance Permit); tier-1 logos (Meta, Switch, Equinix) + NVIDIA validating AI-power demand; near-term isotope revenue optionality (Groves criticality target Jul-4-2026, first contract pending); fuel-flexibility moat-in-the-making (HALEU + recycling + plutonium inventories). RED: $0 revenue vs $10.7B cap; pipeline is non-binding LOIs not contracted backlog; structural dilution (~174M shares +~28% YoY, ATM funding, $350-450M/yr burn); CEO net selling ~$10M; first-power date has slipped before, commercial NRC license pre-submission; stock down ~67% from high yet cap still embeds enormous future success.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

Terminology shift from 'reactor company' to 'energy/fuel platform' (three-unit framing as both genuine diversification AND a narrative hedge against Aurora slippage). DOE pathway as the new hero — increasingly anchored on the May-2025 Trump executive order mandating three test-reactor criticalities by July-4-2026; a politically-supplied, regime-dependent tailwind. 'Shift to the left' on Centrus fuel timing — first explicit acceleration claim, still LOI-grade. The AI partnerships (NVIDIA, Prometheus, Los Alamos) are engineering/modeling collaborations, NOT power-offtake — they burnish the AI association without yet being commercial demand.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

Trajectory: STRENGTHENING — but from a pre-revenue base, so it means the option is getting more valuable and better-funded, not that an earnings base is compounding. The single most important thing to weigh before sizing: this is a pre-revenue, multi-year option priced at $10.7B with continued dilution baked in; nearly all value sits in a terminal-fleet DCF requiring first power on the late-2027 schedule, LOIs converting to priced PPAs, and financing the $350-450M/yr build without crushing per-share value. There is NO margin of safety in the fundamentals — only in how much optionality the -67% drawdown has wrung out. MOAT: NONE->NARROW (prospective) — no deployed asset/returns/margins to certify a moat; the potential moat (regulatory-license + vertical fuel-integration + recurring-PPA switching costs) is real and specific but unbuilt. MOAT_TREND: WIDENING — regulatory lead, fuel agreements and customer LOIs accumulating. SECULAR_THREAT: NONE — Oklo is a secular WINNER (firm carbon-free baseload is exactly what AI buildout is short of); the real risks are execution, dilution, timeline-slip and competition, not terminal-value erosion. The cheapness vs its own high is an execution-risk/de-hype print, not a structurally-shrinking base.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 3 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: WIDENING | SECULAR_THREAT: none
skeptic_kill_fact: No kill_fact found. Every load-bearing number verifies against the Q1-2026 10-Q (period end 3/31/2026): cash+securities $2,536.9M ($1,594.1M cash + $614.5M current + $328.3M non-current marketable securities) = the claimed $2.5B; 173,867,839 actual shares outstanding (~174M, +~28% YoY confirmed); Q1 net loss $33.1M, operating loss $51.2M; $1.18B ATM completed in Q1 (~12.4M shares at avg ~$96.95). Pre-revenue, $0 top line, ~$10.6B cap, spot ~$61 — all confirmed. The DOE Reactor-Pilot July-4-2026 criticality target and late-2027-to-early-2028 first-power date verify (Trump EO-driven, OTA signed; first-of-a-kind slip risk is real). The thesis is a fairly-priced, two-sided pre-revenue option — the B/WATCH-for-capitulation verdict at conviction 3 survives an adversarial attack and is, if anything, conservative.
skeptic_corrections: LOAD-BEARING CORRECTION to the bear case: the debate repeatedly frames the entire ~14 GW pipeline (incl. Meta) as 'entirely NON-BINDING LOIs... no payment obligation.' That is WRONG for the Meta leg. Per Oklo's own newsroom release and the 10-Q, the Oklo-Meta 1.2 GW Pike County, Ohio agreement (signed Jan 5, 2026) is a binding PREPAYMENT AGREEMENT under which Meta PREPAYS for power and FUNDS deployment (Oklo uses the funding to secure nuclear fuel and advance Phase 1) — materially more committed than an LOI, and it carries a payment mechanism the bear thesis denies exists. This correction cuts AGAINST the bear. (The Switch 12 GW DOES remain genuinely non-binding — confirmed 'nonbinding framework' — and Meta is multi-sourcing across Oklo/Vistra/TerraPower, so concentration/conversion risk on the other GW stands.) Second minor correction: Meta first phase is targeted 'as early as 2030' (full 1.2 GW by 2034), separate from and later than the late-2027 INL first-power date — the bull's near-term cash-flow timing leans on INL, not Meta.
skeptic_evidence: Oklo Inc. Form 10-Q for period ended 2026-03-31 (SEC EDGAR, accession 000162828026034095): cash & equivalents $1,594.1M, marketable securities $614.5M current + $328.3M non-current (total cash+securities $2,536.9M), net loss $33.1M, operating loss $51.2M, 173,867,839 shares outstanding, $1.18B 2025-ATM completed in Q1 at avg ~$96.95/sh — https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/0001849056/000162828026034095/oklo-20260331.htm

Oklo newsroom, 2026-01-05: 'Oklo, Meta Announce Agreement in Support of 1.2 GW Nuclear Energy Development in Southern Ohio' — a binding PREPAYMENT agreement (Meta prepays for power + funds deployment), Pike County OH, first phase as early as 2030, full 1.2 GW by 2034 — https://oklo.com/newsroom/news-details/2026/Oklo-Meta-Announce-Agreement-in-Support-of-1-2-GW-Nuclear-Energy-Development-in-Southern-Ohio/default.aspx

Utility Dive, Oklo-Switch 12 GW Master Power Agreement is a NONBINDING framework; Meta inks up to 6.6 GW across Oklo/Vistra/TerraPower (multi-sourcing confirmed) — https://www.utilitydive.com/news/oklo-aurora-smr-advanced-nuclear-reactor-supply-agreement-data-center-developer-switch/735933/

World Nuclear News / Utility Dive (2025): DOE Reactor Pilot Program targets criticality in 3 test reactors by July 4 2026 (Trump EO-driven, Aurora-INL among initial selections, OTA signed); Oklo 75-MW Aurora design, first commercial deployment late-2027 to early-2028 at INL — https://www.utilitydive.com/news/oklo-75-mw-reactor-design-smr-nuclear/743578/

scale.role: Highest-profile SMR pure-play; DOE pilot + July-4-2026 criticality milestone, ~14 GW of non-binding LOIs.
scale.rationale: Real dated regulatory lead and a wide-distribution option, but pre-revenue, base $57 ~at spot, bull +65% / bear -44%. Catalyst SOFT_EXTENDED. The best of the speculative SMRs but still TACTICAL — size as an option, not a CORE holding. Cap 32 honored; conviction 24.
scale.demand_durability: Low-now/high-optional — no revenue yet; LOIs are non-binding and first ops ~late-2027.
